volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Iphigenia among the Taurians (Section 390 - Section 521)

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: ἐς τὴν θεὸν τὸ φαῦλον ἀναφέρειν δοκῶ ·
sections.translation: ascribe to the goddess their sorry behavior . For I believe that no god is evil .

sections.label: Section 1.392
sections.original: κυάνεαι κυάνεαι
sections.translation: Dark straits of the sea , dark , where the gadfly flying from Argos

sections.label: Section 1.395
sections.original: ξενον ἐπʼ οἶδμα διεπέρασεν —
sections.translation: crossed over the inhospitable wave . . . taking the Asian land in exchange for Europe . Whoever are the ones who left the lovely waters

sections.label: Section 1.400
sections.original: λιπόντες Εὐρώταν ἢ
sections.translation: of Eurotas , green with reeds , or the holy streams of Dirce , to come here , to come to the unsociable land , where , for the divine maiden , the blood of mortals stains

sections.label: Section 1.405
sections.original: βωμοὺς καὶ περικίονας
sections.translation: the altars and columned temples ?

sections.label: Section 1.407
sections.original: ἦ ῥοθίοις εἰλατίνας
sections.translation: Did they sail the pine-wood oars with double beat of surge , over the waves of the sea ,

sections.label: Section 1.410
sections.original: ιον ὄχημα λινοπόροις αὔραις ,
sections.translation: a chariot of a ship in breezes that move the linen sails , to increase the contest of wealth for their halls ? For hope is sweet , and

sections.label: Section 1.415
sections.original: ἄπληστος ἀνθρώποις , ὄλ-
sections.translation: insatiable in mortals , to their hurt , for those who bear away the weight of wealth , wandering over the wave and crossing to barbarian cities , with one expectation . But thought of wealth comes at the wrong time for some ,

sections.label: Section 1.420
sections.original: βου , τοῖς δʼ ἐς μέσον ἥκει .
sections.translation: while for others it comes moderately .

sections.label: Section 2.421
sections.original: πῶς πέτρας τὰς συνδρομάδας ,
sections.translation: The rocks that rush together , the sleepless shores of Phineus — how did they cross them , running along the salty coast

sections.label: Section 2.425
sections.original: αἰγιαλὸν ἐπʼ Ἀμφιτρί-
sections.translation: on Amphitrite ’ s surge , where the fifty daughters of Nereus . . . the circular choruses sing ,

sections.label: Section 2.430
sections.original: πλησιστίοισι πνοαῖς
sections.translation: with wind in the sails , the guiding rudder creaking under the stern , with southern breezes or by the blasts of the west wind ,

sections.label: Section 2.435
sections.original: τὰν πολυόρνιθον ἐπʼ αἶ-
sections.translation: to the land of many birds , the white strand , Achilles ’ lovely race-course , over the Black Sea ?

sections.label: Section 2.439
sections.original: εἴθʼ εὐχαῖσιν δεσποσύνοις
sections.translation: Would that , by my mistress ’ prayers , Helen , Leda ’ s dear child ,

sections.label: Section 2.440
sections.original: παῖς ἐλθοῦσα τύχοι τὰν
sections.translation: might happen to leave Troy and come here , where she might die , crowned over her hair by the bloody water ,

sections.label: Section 2.445
sections.original: δεσποίνας χειρὶ θάνοι
sections.translation: her throat cut by the hands of my mistress , and so pay her requital . But what a sweet message I should receive , if a sailor came from Hellas ,

sections.label: Section 2.450
sections.original: δουλείας ἐμέθεν
sections.translation: to put an end to my wretched slavery ! For may I even in dreams be at home and in my ancestral city , the enjoyment of pleasant sleep ,

sections.label: Section 2.455
sections.original: σιν , κοινὰν χάριν ὄλβου .
sections.translation: a grace we have in common with prosperity .

sections.label: Section 456
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἵδε χέρας δεσμοῖς δίδυμοι
sections.translation: But here come the two youths , with tightly bound hands , the new sacrifice for the goddess ; silence , my friends . These first-fruits of Hellas are indeed

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: ναοῖσι πέλας τάδε βαίνει ·
sections.translation: approaching the temple ; the herdman did not deliver a false message .

sections.label: Section 463
sections.original: ὦ πότνιʼ , εἴ σοι τάδʼ ἀρεσκόντως
sections.translation: Lady Artemis , if this city carries out the rites in a way pleasing to you , accept the victims ,

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: ἃς ὁ παρʼ ἡμῖν νόμος οὐχ ὁσίας
sections.translation: which the custom among us declares to be unholy .

sections.label: Section 467
sections.original: εἶἑν ·
sections.translation: Enough ; first , it will be my care to perform well the rites of the goddess . Unbind the strangers ’ hands , so that , as holy victims , they may no longer be in chains .

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: ναοῦ δʼ ἔσω στείχοντες εὐτρεπίζετε
sections.translation: Then go into the temple and make ready what is necessary and customary at the present time .

sections.label: Section 472
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: ( Turning to the prisoners .) Ah ! Who was your mother , who gave you birth , and your father ? And your sister , if you happen to have one . . . such two youths as she has lost ,

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: ἀνάδελφος ἔσται . — τὰς τύχας τίς οἶδʼ ὅτῳ
sections.translation: and will be without a brother ! Who knows where such fortunes will arrive ? For all the gods ’ affairs creep on in darkness , and no one knows evil . . . fate leads us on towards what we cannot know .

sections.label: Section 479
sections.original: πόθεν ποθʼ ἥκετʼ , ὦ ταλαίπωροι ξένοι ;
sections.translation: Unhappy strangers , where have you come from ?

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: ὡς διὰ μακροῦ μὲν τήνδʼ ἐπλεύσατε χθόνα ,
sections.translation: For you have sailed a long time to reach this land , and you will be away from your home a long time , in the world below .

sections.label: Section 482
sections.original: τί ταῦτʼ ὀδύρῃ , κἀπὶ τοῖς μέλλουσι νῷν
sections.translation: Why do you lament these things , and mourn for the evils about to come upon us , lady , whoever you are ? I do not think the one who is about to die wise ,

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: οἴκτῳ τὸ δεῖμα τοὐλέθρου νικᾶν θέλῃ .
sections.translation: if he wishes to conquer the fear of death by wailing , nor the one who laments when Hades is near and there is no hope of safety ; for so he puts together two ills out of one , incurring a charge of folly and dying all the same ; we must let fate alone .

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: ἡμᾶς δὲ μὴ θρήνει σύ · τὰς γὰρ ἐνθάδε
sections.translation: Do not grieve for us ; for we are acquainted with the sacrifices here and we know them .

sections.label: Section 492
sections.original: πότερος ἄρʼ ὑμῶν ἐνθάδʼ ὠνομασμένος
sections.translation: Which of you is called by the name of Pylades ? I want to know this first .

sections.label: Section 494
sections.original: ὅδʼ , εἴ τι δή σοι τοῦτʼ ἐν ἡδονῇ μαθεῖν .
sections.translation: That one , if you have any pleasure in the knowledge .

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: ποίας πολίτης πατρίδος Ἕλληνος γεγώς ;
sections.translation: Of what city of Hellas were you born a citizen ?

sections.label: Section 496
sections.original: τί δʼ ἂν μαθοῦσα τόδε πλέον λάβοις , γύναι ;
sections.translation: What would you gain by learning this , lady ?

sections.label: Section 497
sections.original: πότερον ἀδελφὼ μητρός ἐστον ἐκ μιᾶς ;
sections.translation: Are you brothers , from one mother ?

sections.label: Section 498
sections.original: φιλότητί γʼ · ἐσμὲν δʼ οὐ κασιγνήτω , γύναι .
sections.translation: By friendship , yes ; we are not brothers by birth , lady .

sections.label: Section 499
sections.original: σοὶ δʼ ὄνομα ποῖον ἔθεθʼ ὁ γεννήσας πατήρ ;
sections.translation: What name did your father give you ?

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: τὸ μὲν δίκαιον Δυστυχὴς καλοίμεθʼ ἄν .
sections.translation: I might rightly be called Unfortunate .

sections.label: Section 501
sections.original: οὐ τοῦτʼ ἐρωτῶ · τοῦτο μὲν δὸς τῇ τύχῃ .
sections.translation: I do not ask that ; ascribe that to fortune .

sections.label: Section 502
sections.original: ἀνώνυμοι θανόντες οὐ γελῴμεθʼ ἄν .
sections.translation: If I die unnamed , I would not be mocked at .

sections.label: Section 503
sections.original: τί δὲ φθονεῖς τοῦτο ; ἦ φρονεῖς οὕτω μέγα ;
sections.translation: Why do you begrudge this ? Are you so proud ?

sections.label: Section 504
sections.original: τὸ σῶμα θύσεις τοὐμόν , οὐχὶ τοὔνομα .
sections.translation: You will sacrifice my body , not my name .

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἂν πόλιν φράσειας ἥτις ἐστί σοι ;
sections.translation: Can you not say what city you are from ?

sections.label: Section 506
sections.original: ζητεῖς γὰρ οὐδὲν κέρδος , ὡς θανουμένῳ .
sections.translation: You are seeking nothing profitable , since I am going to die .

sections.label: Section 507
sections.original: χάριν δὲ δοῦναι τήνδε κωλύει τί σε ;
sections.translation: What hinders you from doing me this favor ?

sections.label: Section 508
sections.original: τὸ κλεινὸν Ἄργος πατρίδʼ ἐμὴν ἐπεύχομαι .
sections.translation: The famous Argos I claim as my native land .

sections.label: Section 509
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν , ἀληθῶς , ὦ ξένʼ , εἶ κεῖθεν γεγώς ;
sections.translation: By the gods , truly , stranger , were you born there ?

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: ἐκ τῶν Μυκηνῶν γʼ , αἵ ποτʼ ἦσαν ὄλβιαι .
sections.translation: Yes , from Mycenae , which was once prosperous .

sections.label: Section 511
sections.original: φυγὰς δʼ ἀπῆρας πατρίδος , ἢ ποίᾳ τύχῃ ;
sections.translation: Have you left your country as an exile , or by what fate ?

sections.label: Section 512
sections.original: φεύγω τρόπον γε δή τινʼ οὐχ ἑκὼν ἑκών .
sections.translation: My flight is in some manner willed and unwilled .

sections.label: Section 513
sections.original: ἆρʼ ἄν τί μοι φράσειας ὧν ἐγὼ θέλω ;
sections.translation: Could you then tell me something that I wish to know ?

sections.label: Section 514
sections.original: ὡς ἐν παρέργῳ τῆς ἐμῆς δυσπραξίας .
sections.translation: It will be no great addition to my own misfortune .

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ποθεινός γʼ ἦλθες ἐξ Ἄργους μολών .
sections.translation: Indeed , I am so glad that you have come from Argos !

sections.label: Section 516
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐμαυτῷ γʼ · εἰ δὲ σοί , σὺ τοῦτʼ ἔρα .
sections.translation: I am not ; but if you are , take pleasure in it .

sections.label: Section 517
sections.original: Τροίαν ἴσως οἶσθʼ , ἧς ἁπανταχοῦ λόγος .
sections.translation: Perhaps you know Troy , whose fame is everywhere .

sections.label: Section 518
sections.original: ὡς μήποτʼ ὤφελόν γε μηδʼ ἰδὼν ὄναρ .
sections.translation: Would that I did not , even seen in a dream !

sections.label: Section 519
sections.original: φασίν νιν οὐκέτʼ οὖσαν οἴχεσθαι δορί .
sections.translation: They say it is no more , lost to the spear .

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: ἔστιν γὰρ οὕτως οὐδʼ ἄκραντʼ ἠκούσατε .
sections.translation: It is so ; you have heard nothing that has not happened .

sections.label: Section 521
sections.original: Ἑλένη δʼ ἀφῖκται δῶμα Μενέλεω πάλιν ;
sections.translation: Has Helen come back to Menelaus ’ home ?
